grades.label: Market Position
grades.evidence: ~2.5B daily active users across the Facebook family. Instagram still in growth phase. Number-two share of global digital advertising behind Google.

grades.label: Moat Type & Strength
grades.evidence: Cross-network social graph is the largest in existence. Switching costs at the individual level low, but at the social-circle level very high. 10-K acknowledges regulatory and platform risks at length.

grades.label: Market Quality
grades.evidence: Advertising spend is procyclical; regulatory pressure (FTC inquiry, EU GDPR/DSA), Apple ATT pending, TikTok rising as attention rival. Significant ahead-of-cycle risks visible at vintage date.

grades.label: Leadership Quality
grades.evidence: Zuckerberg founder, super-voting share class. History of large bets (Instagram, WhatsApp acquisitions); willing to spend ahead of revenue (then VR/AR; later AI infrastructure). Long-term framing dominates investor communication.

pullquote: "The engine saw dominant position and wide moat but flagged cyclicality and regulatory headwinds — the calibrated 'good' rather than confident 'great'. Meta drew down ~70% through 2022 before recovering; the realised six-year return sits inside the 'good' bucket."
pullquote_attr: From the demo notebook · 2020 vintage holdout
